edges.description: RyR2-mediated calcium-release susceptibility can promote premature ventricular ectopy capable of triggering short-coupled TdP or VF.
Sodium-channel susceptibility can promote premature ventricular ectopy capable of triggering short-coupled TdP or VF.
The short-coupled ventricular trigger initiates TdP, polymorphic VT, or VF.
Sustained or recurrent polymorphic ventricular tachyarrhythmia can degenerate into VF, producing syncope, aborted sudden cardiac death, or fatal sudden cardiac death.

nodes.description: Short-coupled PVCs initiate torsade de pointes or polymorphic ventricular tachycardia and may degenerate into ventricular fibrillation.

evidence.reference_title: Short-coupled variant of torsade de pointes: A systematic review of case reports and case series
evidence.snippet: The short-coupled variant of torsade de pointes (scTdP) is characterized by a particular electrocardiogram (ECG) pattern that shows a short-coupling interval of the initial Tdp beat and that can degenerate into ventricular fibrillation without the presence of structural heart disease.

evidence.reference_title: A SPRY1 domain cardiac ryanodine receptor variant associated with short-coupled torsade de pointes
evidence.snippet: enhanced store overload-induced Ca2+ release in response to cytosolic Ca2+ was observed in RyR2-I784F cells.

evidence.reference_title: A SPRY1 domain cardiac ryanodine receptor variant associated with short-coupled torsade de pointes
evidence.snippet: The novel RyR2-I784F variant in SPRY1 domain causes a leaky channel under non-stress conditions.

nodes.description: Rare patient-linked RYR2 variants can alter RyR2-mediated calcium release, creating a susceptibility substrate for premature ventricular ectopy in structurally normal hearts.

evidence.reference_title: A SPRY1 domain cardiac ryanodine receptor variant associated with short-coupled torsade de pointes
evidence.snippet: The novel RyR2-I784F variant in SPRY1 domain causes a leaky channel under non-stress conditions.

evidence.reference_title: SCN5A mutation identified in a patient with short-coupled variant of torsades de pointes
evidence.snippet: We identified an SCN5A mutation R800H

evidence.reference_title: SCN5A mutation identified in a patient with short-coupled variant of torsades de pointes
evidence.snippet: showed a significantly shortened recovery time from inactivation.

nodes.description: Rare patient-linked SCN5A variants can alter Nav1.5 recovery from inactivation, creating a candidate sodium-channel susceptibility substrate for short-coupled ventricular ectopy.

evidence.reference_title: SCN5A mutation identified in a patient with short-coupled variant of torsades de pointes
evidence.snippet: showed a significantly shortened recovery time from inactivation.

nodes.description: The defining electrophysiologic event is a premature ventricular contraction with an extremely short coupling interval, often arising from Purkinje or right ventricular endocavitary structures and initiating TdP, polymorphic ventricular tachycardia, or VF.

biological_processes: membrane depolarization during cardiac muscle cell action potential

evidence.reference_title: Short-coupled variant of torsade de pointes. A new electrocardiographic entity in the spectrum of idiopathic ventricular tachyarrhythmias.
evidence.snippet: the torsade had the unusual particularity of an extremely short coupling interval of the first beat or of the isolated premature beats (245 +/- 28 milliseconds)

evidence.reference_title: Catheter ablation of short-coupled variant of torsade de pointes
evidence.snippet: the culprit PVC was mapped to the free wall insertion of the moderator band (MB) with a preceding Purkinje potential in two patients.

nodes.description: Degeneration into ventricular fibrillation produces cardiac arrest or sudden cardiac death unless terminated by defibrillation or an implantable cardioverter-defibrillator.

evidence.reference_title: Short-coupled variant of torsade de pointes. A new electrocardiographic entity in the spectrum of idiopathic ventricular tachyarrhythmias.
evidence.snippet: In 10 cases they deteriorated into ventricular fibrillation.

evidence.reference_title: Short-coupled variant of torsade de pointes. A new electrocardiographic entity in the spectrum of idiopathic ventricular tachyarrhythmias.
evidence.snippet: During a mean follow-up of 7 years there were 5 deaths (4 sudden).
